title: MorphologicVariability: A workflow for quantifying landform morphologic variability via supervised machine learning
description: Python (Jupyter) workflow for quantifying the morphologic variability of linear landforms. The code extracts landform-normal profiles from a DEM, normalizes them, builds a supervised classifier (SVM) over a user-defined set of morphologic classes derived via PCA/SVD on profile shape, and computes a moving-window morphologic variability metric along the landform. Originally developed for the Brigham and Crider (2022) study of fault scarps in jointed bedrock; subsequently applied to Holocene fault scarps in the Stillwater Seismic Gap, Dixie Valley, Nevada.
